title: pilosella fallacina subsp. alsatica
intro: Pilosella fallacina subsp. alsatica, also known as Alsacian mouse-ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion genus. It is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, such as acne and eczema. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. In herbal preparations, Pilosella fallacina subsp. alsatica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally to support skin health and overall well-being.
